blocks.heading: Social media lets people connect online. 📱
blocks.html:
Social media is websites and apps for sharing and talking.

People use it to post, chat, and stay in touch.

blocks.heading: There are different types of social media. 🗂️
blocks.html:
Facebook — share posts and photos with friends.
Instagram — share photos and short videos.
TikTok — share short videos.
Snapchat — send photos and messages that disappear.

blocks.heading: Social media tools share common features. ⚙️
blocks.html:
A profile — your name, photo, and information.
A news feed — posts from people you follow.
Comments and likes — ways to react to posts.
Messaging — sending private messages to others.

blocks.heading: Connectivity means staying linked to others. 🔗
blocks.html:
Social media connects you to friends and family.

You can talk to people anywhere in the world.

blocks.title: Match each social media feature to what it does. 🧩

pairs.definition: Your name, photo, and information on the app
A list of posts from people you follow
Ways to react to someone's post
Sending a private message to one person

options: Websites and apps for sharing and talking
question.explanation: Social media is websites and apps. People use them to post, chat, and stay connected.

question.text: What does your profile show on social media? 👤
options: A list of every website you visit
question.explanation: Your profile shows your name, photo, and basic information about you.

question.text: What does connectivity mean on social media? 🔗
options: How fast your internet loads a page
How many photos you have posted
question.explanation: Connectivity means staying linked to others. Social media connects you to people anywhere.

lessons.title: Pros and Cons of Online Collaboration

blocks.heading: Online collaboration means working together online. 🤝
blocks.html:
You can chat, share files, and plan with others.

You do not need to be in the same room.

blocks.heading: Online collaboration has good points. ✅
blocks.html:
Better connectivity — you stay in touch with people far away.
More engagement — more people can join in and share ideas.
Quick to share files, photos, and updates.

blocks.heading: Online collaboration also has risks. ⚠️
blocks.html:
You may share your personal information by accident.
Apps can show your physical location to others.
Strangers may see things you only meant for friends.

blocks.heading: Be careful what you share online. 📍
blocks.html:
Sharing your location can tell strangers where you are.

Sharing personal details can put your safety at risk.

blocks.title: Sort each statement into Advantage or Disadvantage. 📊

categories.label: Advantage ✅
items: Stay connected with people far away
More people can join in and share ideas

categories.label: Disadvantage ⚠️
items: Your location may be shared with strangers
Personal information can leak by accident

question.explanation: Online collaboration means working together using the internet, even if you are far apart.

question.text: Which is a disadvantage of online collaboration? ⚠️
options: You can chat with people far away
Your personal information or location may be shared
question.explanation: A risk of online collaboration is accidentally sharing personal information or your location.

options: More people join in and share ideas
question.explanation: Increased engagement means more people are joining in, posting, and sharing ideas together.

blocks.heading: Ethical behaviour means doing the right thing. ✅
blocks.html:
This is true online too, not just in person.

Ethical online behaviour means being kind, honest, and respectful.

blocks.heading: Appropriate online behaviour looks like this. 👍
blocks.html:
Being polite and respectful in comments and messages.
Asking before sharing photos of other people.
Thinking before you post — is it true and is it kind?

blocks.heading: Inappropriate online behaviour looks like this. 👎
blocks.html:
Posting mean comments or bullying someone.
Sharing private photos without asking.
Spreading rumours or lies about someone.

blocks.heading: Your digital reputation is what people think of you online. 🏷️
blocks.html:
Everything you post builds your digital reputation.

Bad posts can follow you for years, even if you delete them.

blocks.heading: Inappropriate behaviour has real consequences. ⚠️
blocks.html:
Digital reputation — others may see you as unkind or untrustworthy.
Relationships — friends and family may lose trust in you.
Employment — employers often check your social media before hiring.

blocks.title: Sort each action as Appropriate or Inappropriate. 🧩

categories.label: Appropriate 👍
items: Asking before sharing a friend's photo

options: What people think of you based on what you post online
The number of apps you have installed
question.explanation: Your digital reputation is what people think of you based on what you post and do online.

question.text: Why might inappropriate posts affect your employment? 💼
options: Employers often check social media before hiring someone
question.explanation: Many employers check a person's social media before offering them a job.

question.text: Which of these is appropriate online behaviour? 👍
options: Sharing a friend's photo without asking
question.explanation: Thinking before you post helps you avoid hurting others or damaging your reputation.

blocks.heading: Privacy means keeping your information safe. 🔒
blocks.html:
Privacy is your right to control who sees your information.

Online, this includes your photos, messages, and location.

blocks.heading: Protect your privacy online. 🛡️
blocks.html:
Use privacy settings to control who sees your posts.
Do not share your address or phone number publicly.
Think twice before posting your location.

blocks.heading: A code of conduct sets the rules for behaviour. 📋
blocks.html:
A code of conduct is a list of rules for how to behave.

Schools and workplaces use them to keep everyone safe and respectful.

blocks.heading: Why do schools and workplaces need an ICT code of conduct? 🏫
blocks.html:
It tells people how to use computers and the internet properly.
It protects everyone's privacy and safety.
It explains what happens if someone breaks the rules.

blocks.title: Spot the phishing email. 📧
blocks.instruction: Read each email and decide if it is safe or a scam trying to steal your privacy.

emails.subject: Your account will be locked! Verify now
emails.body: We noticed unusual activity. Click this link and enter your password within 24 hours or your account will be deleted.

redFlags.detail: Says your account will be deleted to scare you
Real companies never ask for your password by email

emails.subject: Reminder: ICT code of conduct training next week
emails.body: Hi students, just a reminder that our ICT code of conduct session is on Tuesday in the library. No action needed.

emails.subject: You won a $500 gift card! Claim your prize
emails.body: Congratulations! Enter your full name, address, and phone number here to claim your free gift card before it expires.

redFlags.detail: Random prizes you did not enter are a common scam
Wants your address and phone number
freegiftcardz.net is not a trusted company

blocks.title: Put these steps for protecting your privacy online in order. 🔢
orderedItems: Choose who can see your posts
Avoid sharing your address or phone number

options: Controlling who can see your information
Deleting all your social media accounts
question.explanation: Privacy means you control who can see your information, like photos and location.

question.text: What is a code of conduct? 📋
options: A list of rules for how to behave
question.explanation: A code of conduct is a list of rules. Schools and workplaces use it to guide behaviour.

question.text: Why do schools have an ICT code of conduct? 🏫
options: To keep everyone safe and using technology properly
To stop students from using computers
question.explanation: An ICT code of conduct keeps people safe and explains how to use technology properly.
